rulings.Case Title: JON DOE JR VS. CHARLES AKHIDNEOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preclusionary Sanctions For Missing The Deadline For Filing The Administrative Record
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF JON DOE's Motion For Preclusionary Sanctions For Missing The Deadline For Filing The Administrative Record: Hearing required to address Respondent's compliance with CCP 1094.6(d). (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TESHOME T TAREKEGN, VS. FREWWORK ABERRA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT ASNAKECH BELEW'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: Sustained with 10 day leave to amend to allege a viable cause of action against the moving defendant and to allege fraud with particularity. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Reconsider And/Or Revoke Prior Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFF MICKEY WONG's Motion to Reconsider And/Or Revoke Prior Order: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008. No new facts, circumstance or law presented. Document presented in support of the motion have been in Plaintiff's possession at the time First through Fourth Amended Complaint were filed. (501/REQ) - Late Tentative

rulings.Case Title: SOUTH COUNTY BANK, N.A. VS. FIRST-CITIZENS BANK & TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BANKERS BANCSHARES' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES: GRANTED. Motion is timely. Defendant shall recover from plaintiff attorney's fees in the amount of $19,438.35. (501/REQ) - Late Tentative

rulings.Case Title: SOUTH COUNTY BANK, N.A. VS. FIRST-CITIZENS BANK & TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF SOUTH COUNTY BANK's Motion To Tax Costs: DENIED. Kaufman v. Diskeeper Corporation (2014 DJDAR 11468) (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SOUTH COUNTY BANK, N.A. VS. FIRST-CITIZENS BANK & TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Judgment Entered June 30, 2014
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF SOUTH COUNTY BANK's Motion To Vacate Judgment Entered June 30, 2014: GRANTED. Judgment entered on June 30, 2014 is hereby vacated. Order of April 23, 2014 is considered a final judgment in this action by operation of CCP 581d. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAZHEN LLC DBA BLUSH MASSAGE THERAPY & SPA VS. OK INVESTMENTS INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTS OK INVESTMENTS INC and HOMAYOUN NAIMI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: Continued to October 16, 2014. Opposing party is ordered to comply SFLR to 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition no later than September 19, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint no later than September 19, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAZHEN LLC DBA BLUSH MASSAGE THERAPY & SPA VS. OK INVESTMENTS INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTS OK INVESTMENTS INC and HOMAYOUN NAIMI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: continued to October 16, 2014. Opposing party is ordered to comply SFLR to 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition no later than September 19, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint no later than September 19, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALVIN BENJAMIN CHONG VS. AARON BARTON CHONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT AARON CHONG'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE: transferred to department 204 to be heard with the pending motion to Change Venue in the related case (PTR-11-295018) on September 24, 2014. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALVIN BENJAMIN CHONG VS. AARON BARTON CHONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT AARON CHONG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Continued to October 22, 2014 pending decision on the Motions to Change Venue. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AXIS DEVELOPMENT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. 1436 STEVENSON, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS RPS14, 14FJL LLC, and 1463 STEVENSON LLC'S Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action: GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to show reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits. Parties' agreement did not require seller's written acceptance of the buyer's discretionary right under the contract to cancel the agreement. Defendant's request for attorney's fees and costs granted in the amount of $10,982. Pages 16-27 of Plaintiff's opposition are stricken. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BLANCA L. HOLCOMB VS. JULIO R. MENDIOLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 8: PLAINTIFF BLANCA HOLCOMB'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant: GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide responses to Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer and Special Interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $850. (501/REQ)
